senses.gloss: to strike, to beat, to hit

examples.english: He hit his head against the wall.

senses.gloss: to shoot at, to fire at, to bombard
to type (on a keyboard or typewriter)

examples.text: ضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الذِّلَّةُ أَيْنَ مَا ثُقِفُوا إِلَّا بِحَبْلٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَحَبْلٍ مِنَ النَّاسِ
examples.english: Ignominy shall be their portion wheresoever they are found save (where they grasp) a rope from Allah and a rope from men.

senses.gloss: to leave, to abandon, to turn away from, to forsake
to coin, to mint, to strike
to cast, to throw, to set, to put

examples.english: he threw the snare over the bird
he set a barrier between (the two of) them

senses.gloss: to set or posit as an example or make a simile of, to be proverbial with

examples.text:
خِلَاط
بكَسر أوله، وآخره طاء مهملة: بلدة عامرة مشهورة كثيرة الخيرات، وهى قصبة أرمينية الوسطى، يضرب ببردها فى الشتاء المثل، وبُحَيْرَتُها يُجلَبُ منها السمك الطّرّيخ، [على وزن سكّين] ^(«٣») ، ليس فى غيرها، يُحمَلُ إلى سائرِ البلاد البعيدة، وهى من العجب، فإنها عشرة أشهر لا يوجد فيها حيوان، لا سمك ولا غيره، ثم يَظهر بها السمك مدة شهرين، فيُصاد ويكبس.
examples.english: Xlat: A flourishing, famous town, full of amenities, it is a fortress of Central Armenia, and its burd clothing sets an example in winter, and there is Lake Van whence one imports the pearl mullet, to which there is no equal, which is carried to the rest of the towns far away, it’s wonderful, ten months the animal is not found therein, no fish nor anything, then there appears fish for two months, and is caught and pickled.

senses.gloss: to pulsate, to throb, to beat (of a vein or the heart)
to ache violently, to hurt severely (of a wound or tooth)
to incline, to tend, to shade

examples.text: قرنوة: الغافقي: قال قوم أنها الهرنوة والقرنوة أيضاً حشيشة. […] قال أبو حنيفة: هي عشبة يضرب ورقها إلى الحمرة وهي مرة يدبغ بها. وقال أيضاً عن بعضهم: هي خضراء غبراء على ساق لها ثمرة كالسنبلة ومنابتها السهول وهي مرعى. وقال آخر: القرنوة عشبة يطول ورقها كورق الحندقوقا عفصة تستعمل في دباغة الجلود وقيل أنها هذه الحشيشة المعروفة بالأنجبار.
examples.english: Aloeswood. Al-Ġāfaqīy: Some say that these seeds of aloe are a herb. […] Abū Ḥanīfa: The tender leaves of this herb tend towards red and this is when they are used for tanning. And some authors said they are dingy towards the foot and have fruits like ears like spikes growing on plains and used for pasturage. Another said: It’s a plant of long leaves like honey lotus the gall of which is used for tanning hides and it has been said it is a herb known as al-anjabār.

senses.gloss: to get the better of in beating
to be smitten by hoarfrost or rime
to give a violent hit, give a sound blow
to sew on a lining or padding
to drink of the milk called ضَرِيب (ḍarīb, “milk of several camels in the same bucket”)
to get overtaken by the snow (ضَرِيب (ḍarīb, “snow, hoarfrost, ice”))
verbal noun of ضَرَبَ (ḍaraba, “to beat”) (form I)
last foot of the second hemistich
of a slender make and not fleshy
